canto_title: The Encounter with Manfred and the Limits of Reason

scenes.summary: Dante reflects on his fear of being lost and his sense of conscience.
Dante describes his physical movement and the sight of the mountain ascending toward the sky.
Virgil explains that the sun is obscured by the shadow of the mountain.
Virgil discusses the impossibility of human reason grasping the mysteries of the Trinity.
The travelers reach the base of the mountain and find a difficult, broken path.
A group of souls moves toward the travelers, prompting Dante to suggest Virgil speak to them.
The approaching souls cluster closely against the rocks of the mountain.
Virgil calls out to the souls, asking for the way to the summit.
Dante compares the modest, timid movement of the souls to a flock of sheep.
The souls react with uncertainty to the sudden darkness caused by the passing shadow.
The souls explain that the sun's light is blocked by the mountain's wall.
A beautiful, wounded soul signals the travelers and draws their attention.
Dante greets the man, who shows him a wound on his chest.
The soul identifies himself as Manfred and describes his violent death.
Manfred recounts his death in Benevento and the enduring power of divine love.
Manfred explains the punishment for those who die in contumacy against the Church.
Manfred asks Dante to deliver a message to his daughter, Constance.
